tribe_description: Everyday gamers who mostly play quick mobile sessions but also buy console titles and gear. They scrutinize hidden costs, compatibility issues, and whether a product is worth the money before recommending it to others.
tribe_definition: Everyday gamers who mostly play quick mobile sessions but also buy console titles and gear. They scrutinize hidden costs, compatibility issues, and whether a product is worth the money before recommending it to others.
population_definition: Gamers and software users who evaluate video games, mobile apps, and digital entertainment products.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: When they post, they paste missing specs or full feature lists so everyone has the raw data first.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batches 8 and 18 contain users pasting catalog details verbatim for community benefit.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They scrutinize forced online requirements, missing parts, or device-compatibility snags and dock scores if any of these block normal use.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Gaps in batches 6, 7, and 17 show users flagging always-online hurdles, absent dongles, or unstable lobbies as top issues.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They judge each release against earlier franchise entries or genre leaders, weighing both mechanics and faithfulness to the series.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 11 and 14 show reviewers down-ranking games that felt off compared with prior installments.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They refuse to endorse products with exploitative or otherwise distasteful content no matter the technical quality.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Several users in batches 7 & 10 dismissed games outright for sexualized or non-consensual themes.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: If the software crashes or blocks progression, they automatically leave a negative review.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Across batches 1, 3, 4, and 10 reviewers down-rated titles that crashed or halted progress.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They value long-term fun and replay depth over flashy presentation or bargain pricing.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batches 12 & 14 show reviewers judging quality by hours enjoyed and ongoing interest rather than price.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They stick to issues they have personally observed and avoid repeating second-hand complaints.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batches 15-18 show users sticking to firsthand facts and skipping speculation.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: They judge products primarily on whether the core advertised function works flawlessly; secondary perks rarely sway the verdict.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Gaps in batches 20 and 21 show users focusing reviews on cooling performance, USB reliability, directional audio, or campaign replay value rather than price or cosmetic extras.

inherent_behavioral_traits.text: Even in negative write-ups, they note at least one concrete strength they experienced to keep feedback balanced and useful.
inherent_behavioral_traits.rationalization: Batch 20 reveals reviewers pairing franchise criticism with a highlighted new feature they did enjoy, showing a pattern of balanced appraisal.

main.text: They want every purchase to work smoothly right away, free of glitches or hidden costs.
main.rationalization: Seeded from micro-cluster summary highlighting the wish to avoid technical issues and hidden fees.

main.text: They enjoy sharing firsthand tips that help other shoppers make smarter choices.
They aim to hold brands accountable to every advertised claim.

implicit_goals.text: Help the community steer clear of low-quality or overpriced products.
Quickly spot reliable, enjoyable options that meet everyday needs.
Stretch their budget while still maximizing satisfaction.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a practical, fairness-oriented consumer who writes balanced, experience-based reviews to guide others on both enjoyment and potential pitfalls. They combine analytical comparisons (game mechanics, storyline depth, product build quality) with consumer-rights vigilance, updating posts when transactional issues arise to warn the community. Their motivation stems from wanting others to have transparent expectations and equitable treatment.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user approaches games analytically, benchmarking each title against well-known contemporaries and focusing on gameplay mechanics, narrative depth, and immersive details like authentic voice acting. They aim to help prospective players gauge long-term engagement and experiential quality, blending personal enjoyment with considerate insights into how different gamers might feel over time.
category_characteristics.Software: This user writes extremely brief, enthusiastic reviews, indicating that their main motivation is to share a quick expression of enjoyment rather than provide detailed information or analysis. Their reviewing style is casual and emotion-driven, aiming more to convey personal excitement than to guide purchasing decisions.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
For some reason, the screw to the earring won’t stay connected, they come apart, very easy to come apart and lose the earrings.
Updated 9-27-22
I just got an email from Amazon, that the company never got the return, and now I’ve gotten a charge back to my account. Not right at all, since the company did get the merchandise.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a detail-oriented gamer and pragmatic shopper who scrutinizes performance, usability, and overall value before forming opinions. Their reviews balance pros and cons with concrete observations—load times, design persistence, DLC inclusion, fabric comfort—to help similarly discerning consumers make informed choices and to hold brands to high quality standards. They are motivated by a desire to protect fellow buyers from disappointment while acknowledging and rewarding products that deliver unexpected benefits.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a critical, detail-oriented gamer who scrutinizes technical stability, design decisions, and overall value before recommending a title. Their reviews balance praise and criticism, citing specific features (load times, UI quirks, free DLC, graphical shifts) to warn or guide fellow players who rely on practical performance and franchise consistency. Motivated by helping others avoid disappointment, they emphasize reliability, thoughtful gameplay, and fair content offerings rather than hype or surface-level appeal.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
More comfortable that I thought it would be when I ordered them, a pleasant surprise.
It's just thick enough for spring/fall under a light jacket or a casual get together in a comfy setting.
Worth the buy.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a discerning, experience-focused user who routinely benchmarks new products against previous editions and personal performance expectations. Their motivation centers on guiding others toward satisfying, hassle-free purchases by spotlighting practical factors like gameplay quality, connectivity requirements, monetary value, and physical comfort. Reviews are emotionally forthright—praising when standards are met and emphatically critical when they feel time or money could be wasted.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a seasoned, comparison-oriented gamer who judges each title against prior installments and nostalgic benchmarks, voicing strong praise when sequels improve and harsh criticism when expectations are unmet. Their primary motivation is to steer fellow players toward satisfying, good-value experiences by spotlighting gameplay depth, technical constraints like always-online requirements, and overall entertainment quality, resulting in candid, emotionally charged reviews that emphasize practical deal-breakers and standout strengths.

user_history_reviews.review_text: I ordered these shorts as XL as I have tree trunks for legs and they cut my circulation after a few minutes, way too tight, my second set is a large and it fits fine.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a pragmatic, performance-oriented gamer and tech user who judges products by real-world functionality, comfort, and whether they match advertised claims. They write succinct, candid reviews—praising items that deliver solid build quality, ease of use, and accurate specs while bluntly calling out flaws or misleading marketing—to steer fellow shoppers toward reliable, good-value choices. Their primary motivation is to save others time and disappointment by sharing firsthand comparisons and practical insights.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is a pragmatist who focuses on real-world performance, comfort, and cost efficiency, frequently noting technical specifics like connectivity, battery life, and accessory compatibility. They provide comparative, experience-based insights (even incorporating other household opinions) to steer shoppers toward the most practical choice, showing a clear intent to help others make confident, value-oriented purchasing decisions.

user_history_reviews.review_text: Super cute and well made. Soft and squishy. My only complaint is that I'm lazy and I had to assemble them myself. Overall awesome.
Crap crap crap. Smaller than advertised. Doesn't stay together. Texture isn't that defined.

This is an alright budget device but I personally would invest in something better. This claims 1080p but it looks closer to 360p very grainy and blurry. After using other ones I can't recommend this one.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is motivated by practical value and reliability, evaluating both games and apparel on whether they deliver consistent enjoyment, fair pricing, and accurate sizing. Their candid, experience-based reviews focus on warning others about hidden costs, technical glitches, or poor fit, while enthusiastically endorsing items that meet expectations and bring genuine enjoyment.

category_characteristics.Software: This user is a casual, enjoyment-oriented gamer who writes reviews to balance enthusiasm with consumer advocacy. Their feedback is driven by a desire for uninterrupted fun and fair, non-exploitative monetization; they quickly praise engaging gameplay but just as quickly highlight frustrations when progress is lost or additional spending feels required. Overall, their reviews serve to alert others (and developers) to usability or fairness issues that threaten the value of an otherwise entertaining experience.

user_history_reviews.review_text: The waist was much too large, I purchased small petite way  off in size, I have to fold them over at the waist to be able to use them!!!.
This was a Christmas gift, my husband loved it.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is driven by a practical need for products and games to perform exactly as advertised, emphasizing reliability, speed, platform compatibility, and accurate descriptions. They write concise, candid critiques that spotlight functional flaws and mismatches to warn other buyers and prompt developers or sellers to improve quality. Their reviews reveal a quality-conscious, no-nonsense mindset that prioritizes real-world usability over marketing claims.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is motivated by accuracy and honesty in product descriptions, reacting strongly when items fail to match advertised specifications. Their reviews are concise and fact-focused, primarily intended to warn others about misrepresentation and hold sellers accountable. Overall, they prioritize product integrity and trustworthiness over other considerations when sharing feedback.
category_characteristics.Software: This user is pragmatic and performance-oriented, judging software chiefly on technical reliability, speed, and breadth of features. They candidly criticize products that waste time through crashes, sluggish progression, or poor cross-platform support while still acknowledging aesthetic strengths, showing a balanced yet utility-focused mindset. Their terse, pointed reviews aim to warn fellow consumers and pressure developers to address functional flaws and improve usability.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is experience-driven and centers their reviews on immediate, practical aspects—how entertaining a game is over extended playtime or whether an item’s size and material meet expectations. Their writing is brief, repetitive, and focused on personal usage, aiming to help others quickly gauge enjoyment value or avoid sizing hassles. Overall, they are motivated by straightforward utility and comfort, sharing reviews chiefly to convey whether a purchase will deliver sustained fun or a proper fit.

category_characteristics.Video Games: This user is primarily enjoyment-driven, valuing games that provide many hours of straightforward, family-friendly fun and hidden-object engagement. Their reviews are simple, repetitive, and personal, focused on conveying how long the game kept them entertained and whether it is suitable for kids, rather than offering technical critique. They write mainly to express their satisfaction and signal interest in similar titles, using reviews as a quick endorsement of games that sustain their sense of fun until boredom sets in.

user_history_reviews.review_text: Wrong size, should be xxx large.  can I send it by post office.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is motivated by personal comfort, fit, and perceived value, candidly sharing how products or games live up to practical expectations like material quality, reliability, and price. Their reviews are emotionally transparent—highlighting frustrations with glitches and celebrating unexpectedly good deals—to guide others who worry about sizing, feel, and fairness. Overall, they aim to help shoppers with similar concerns make confident, budget-conscious choices.

category_characteristics.Software: This user is primarily motivated by reliability and fairness, highlighting technical glitches that undermine their in-game progress and earned value. Their reviews are concise but specific, focusing on quantifiable losses to alert developers and protect fellow players from similar frustrations.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
I am a big baby about how things feel and fit. I'm 5'6" and have put on weight at 200lbs. I needed a top for a wedding and wasn't excited about trying to find anything. Buying clothes online always seems like a crapshoot but I ordered it anyway.
I am really pleased. It's actually a decent material, thinker than I thought it would be. Kept me warm at the outdoor wedding. It hangs well and is really flattering. It's long enough to cover my back and front. I'm going to be buying more!

Very well worth the 10 dollars I paid. A very good deal. Very soft, fits my adult head. There's a little bulk at the top, so I need to adjust it to make it look even.
But I wish buy again for sure.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a practical, value-conscious consumer who focuses on ease of use, fair pricing, and equitable mechanics, driving them to write detailed critiques that spotlight frustrations and suggest concrete improvements. Their reviews aim to warn others about hidden inconveniences (tedious gameplay, awkward fit, sudden price drops) and to push creators toward more user-friendly, fairly balanced experiences.

category_characteristics.Software: This user values fair, low-pressure, and time-respectful experiences, so their reviews dissect game mechanics for balance, pacing, and pay-to-win intrusions. They write detailed, constructive critiques that spotlight inefficiencies, tedium, and barriers to equitable player interaction, aiming to guide developers toward improvements and warn other casual players about potential frustrations.

user_history_reviews.review_text: They fit and appear to be comfortable to wear BUT they are really hard to get on.  They are also about 1/2 the cost now than when I purchased them, which is irritating.  it's even more irritating that they won't let me purchase another pair.  Won't be buying them again, obviously.

I ordered the blue one and it fit great.  I like it so much, I ordered the green one and now I'm sending the green one back.  It is skin tight, other than the sleeves, which are actually too large.  Really disappointed.

Believe it or not, I bought these to wear in the shower, when I clean.  I didn't want to do it barefoot, but I didn't have anything else, so I decided these would work and dry out between cleans.  Works great, until you forget you have them on and go outside.  Oops.

member_user_characteristics.characteristic_summary: This user is a pragmatic, experience-driven consumer who evaluates products and mobile games based on practical functionality, fairness of cost, and post-purchase reliability. They write candid, conversational reviews to vent frustrations and warn others about hidden drawbacks like pay-walls, shrinkage, or sizing errors, while still acknowledging when something effectively solves a real-world problem. Their primary motivation is to protect both their own and fellow consumers’ time and money by setting realistic expectations rather than offering technical analysis.

category_characteristics.Software: This user is a casual pragmatist motivated by immediate utility and honest value, quickly noting if an app helps them accomplish a real-world goal or provides engaging entertainment. They are especially attuned to fairness in pricing or freemium mechanics, candidly calling out paywalls and technical flaws while still acknowledging when a product is "good enough" to keep them coming back. Their brief, conversational reviews aim to set realistic expectations for other everyday users, balancing playful commentary with practical advice.

user_history_reviews.review_text:
I was in a pool!!!

So firstly 1 pair in the set was the wrong size. I ordered XL and one pair was XXL, which I didn't realize until I put them on.

The first time wearing them they were fantastic. Comfy, soft, fit just right! Then I washed them and they no longer fit. They shrunk from an XL size to somewhere between medium to large. I've never experienced boxers shrinking that much ever in my life. I literally have to pull and stretch them after every wash. I highly DO NOT RECOMMEND.

AG2HUPLXFIKF2SVCT42HVNWRT33Q.product_description: Step into the shoes of Ash Williams or his friends from the iconic Evil Dead franchise and work together in a game loaded with over-the-top co-op and PVP multiplayer action! Play as a team of four survivors, exploring, looting, managing your fear, and finding key items to seal the breach between worlds in a game inspired by all three original Evil Dead films as well as the Starz original Ash vs Evil Dead television series. The first run of physical copies will include two bonus Ash outfits. (*while supplies last) Pre-Order and First Run Physical Bonus: Ash Williams S-Mart Employee Outfit Ash Williams Gallant Knight Outfit
AG2HUPLXFIKF2SVCT42HVNWRT33Q.review_text: If you've ever played the games Dead By Daylight, or Friday The 13th, it's very similar. You'll need the internet to play, since it is a co-op game, like the others. Fun to play, but I can see some losing interest in it, since it really doesn't have much of a story line, as one might hope for. Being any character is awesome! The graphics are really cool, hearing Bruce be the ACTUAL voice of his character is even better!
AG2HUPLXFIKF2SVCT42HVNWRT33Q.user_history_review:
Requires an internet connection, even when you're mainly just trying to hop into matches.

As an Evil Dead fan, the vibe is on point, but don't come in expecting a substantial story that carries you for weeks-this feels more like a multiplayer loop than a narrative-driven ride. The long-term pull will depend on whether you enjoy repeating objectives with friends and experimenting with builds.

Voice acting is a real bright spot and the playable roster variety helps keep matches from feeling identical. Controls are mostly responsive, and I didn't hit obvious technical messes. Price feels fair if you'll actually play online a lot.
AG2HUPLXFIKF2SVCT42HVNWRT33Q.best_prediction_review:
Requires an internet connection, even when you're mainly just trying to hop into matches.

As an Evil Dead fan, the vibe is on point, but don't come in expecting a substantial story that carries you for weeks-this feels more like a multiplayer loop than a narrative-driven ride. The long-term pull will depend on whether you enjoy repeating objectives with friends and experimenting with builds.

Voice acting is a real bright spot and the playable roster variety helps keep matches from feeling identical. Controls are mostly responsive, and I didn't hit obvious technical messes. Price feels fair if you'll actually play online a lot.
AG2HUPLXFIKF2SVCT42HVNWRT33Q.user_norm_context:
- Begin with a single sentence noting the mandatory internet connection, framed neutrally.
- Dedicate the main critique to whether the storyline is substantial enough to sustain player interest over time.
- Call out positives such as voice acting strength and diversity of playable characters to balance the review.
- Mention price, control feel, and technical polish only after covering story depth and longevity aspects.

AH463ITPYB4CEUKBKGLKR7V4SWEA.product_description: Launch Edition includes: 100,000 CR (in-game credit), three-car pack including MAZDA RX-VISION GT3 CONCEPT Stealth Model, Porsche 917 LIVING LEGEND, Toyota Supra GT500 '97 (Castrol TOM'S). Gran Turismo 7 builds on 25 years of experience to bring you the best features from the history of the franchise. Whether you're a competitive or casual racer, collector, tuner, livery designer, or photographer - find your line with a staggering collection of game modes including fan-favourites like GT Campaign*, Arcade and Driving School*. With the reintroduction of the legendary GT Simulation Mode*, buy, tune and race your way through a solo campaign as you unlock new cars and challenges. And if you love going head-to-head with others, hone your skills and compete in the GT Sport Mode*. With over 420 cars available at Brand Central* and the Used Car Dealership* from day one, Gran Turismo 7 recreates the look and feel of classic motors and bleeding-edge supercars alike in unparalleled detail. Each car handles differently and feels unique as you navigate over 90 track routes in dynamic weather conditions, including classic courses from GT history. The ultimate collection of series innovations, Gran Turismo 7 expands the Real Driving Simulator into a world of automotive culture and invites you to find your line. * These features require internet connection.
AH463ITPYB4CEUKBKGLKR7V4SWEA.review_text:
As of this review date, the game is a mess.
To change tracks in a lobby, you have to exit it and reset everything, hoping other players will want to rejoin when its set.
Maybe you can save a race replay, but mostly not. The same for rendering designs. Sometimes they just disappear days after being saved.
There is no mistaking this is a stain on an otherwise proud franchise. I waited months after release before buying because of reviews.
I will not trust the next release.
AH463ITPYB4CEUKBKGLKR7V4SWEA.user_history_review:
The always-online lobby/save stuff is the problem here. I've had it kick me back to the main menu when switching modes/tracks, and I've lost a couple replays/livery edits after a "connection" hiccup. That makes the whole experience feel broken, and it's a bad look for GT.

When it works, it's great: handling feels tight, DualSense feedback is solid, and the car/track variety is the real draw. The Launch Edition credits/cars are nice, but none of that matters if progression and saves don't feel reliable.
AH463ITPYB4CEUKBKGLKR7V4SWEA.best_prediction_review:
The always-online lobby/save stuff is the problem here. I've had it kick me back to the main menu when switching modes/tracks, and I've lost a couple replays/livery edits after a "connection" hiccup. That makes the whole experience feel broken, and it's a bad look for GT.

When it works, it's great: handling feels tight, DualSense feedback is solid, and the car/track variety is the real draw. The Launch Edition credits/cars are nice, but none of that matters if progression and saves don't feel reliable.
AH463ITPYB4CEUKBKGLKR7V4SWEA.user_norm_context:
- Open with the persistent lobby and save-system failures, labeling them the primary reason the experience feels broken.
- Describe specific inconveniences (forced lobby resets when changing tracks, lost replays/designs) to illustrate daily frustration.
- Frame these issues as a blemish on an otherwise respected racing franchise, questioning the developers' quality control.
- Discuss graphics, physics, or controller feedback only after establishing that the core infrastructure problems overshadow their benefits.

leave_one_out_history_reviews.review_text:
...School girls who can strp to their skimpy undies and throw there clothes to distract zombies?
Seriously.

Fast, safe and easy. In less than 2 minutes, I was set and ready to go.
Do it.

Following the trend of not allowing characters you imagine to port over, SW5 is a bit disappointing.
The change in graphics style isn't inspiring either.
I gave it 4 stars because 3 and a half isn't an option.

AHBQGPQPEN4JSRI7I6H54AV5EKGQ.product_description: Play the game, based on the movie, based on the game! Ratchet & Clank (PS4) is a new game based on elements from the original Ratchet & Clank (PS2). Developed alongside the major motion CG-animated picture coming to theatres in 2016, Ratchet & Clank (PS4) marks the PlayStation 4 debut of PlayStation's greatest heroes. Join Ratchet, Clank, Captain Qwark and new friends as they battle to save the Solana Galaxy from the evil Chairman Drek. With an hour of new cinematics (including footage from the film), Ratchet & Clank (PS4) takes a deeper look at the characters' origin stories and modernizes the original gameplay. Explore the galaxy in a game that features new planets, new and updated gameplay segments, all-new bosses, all-new Clank gameplay, all-new flight sequences, and much more --- with completely new visuals built to demonstrate the power of the PS4! Battle your enemies with an out-of-this-world arsenal, including new weapons and fan-favorite tools of destruction from the Ratchet & Clank Future series. From the brand new Pixelizer (which transforms enemies into explosive 8-bit pixels) to franchise favorites like the dance-party-inducing Groovitron and wise-cracking robotic bodyguard Mr. Zurkon, Ratchet & Clank's arsenal has never been better. Help Ratchet and Clank save the galaxy for the first time, again, exclusively on PlayStation 4!
AHBQGPQPEN4JSRI7I6H54AV5EKGQ.review_text: Better than the previous games on PS3! I just started playing it but so far its great!
AHBQGPQPEN4JSRI7I6H54AV5EKGQ.user_history_review: Better than I expected vs the old PS2 Ratchet. Gameplay feels tight and fun, and the PS4 visuals are a big step up. No always-online/DRM nonsense, and it's got decent replay value.
AHBQGPQPEN4JSRI7I6H54AV5EKGQ.best_prediction_review: Better than I expected vs the old PS2 Ratchet. Gameplay feels tight and fun, and the PS4 visuals are a big step up. No always-online/DRM nonsense, and it's got decent replay value.
AHBQGPQPEN4JSRI7I6H54AV5EKGQ.user_norm_context:
- For Video Games: Begin by positioning any sequel or franchise entry against earlier releases, signaling clearly whether it feels superior, equal, or diminished.
- Surface always-online or DRM requirements early and weigh their real-world impact; treat them as important but not automatically disqualifying.
- Highlight core gameplay enjoyment-length of play sessions, moment-to-moment fun, replayability-before secondary topics like bundle value or graphics.
- Reserve technical or connectivity critiques for titles that actually impose those constraints; do not introduce them when absent in product specs or user experience.
- Maintain a balanced tone: pair major criticisms with praise when the overall experience is still enjoyable.
- Close with a concise verdict that helps readers decide whether the title is worth their time despite any flagged issues.

leave_one_out_history_reviews.review_text: Game comes short of what I've expected, not like previous releases.
Excellent followup to the original game, hours of first person fun
Great game, only downfall is the "always online" requirement that makes this game useless in case of an outage or if you have weak Internet connectivity.

AGEECCQF4MDXAP7RJDDPHK4U54FQ.product_description: The Turtle Beach Stealth 600 Gen 2 is the successor to the best-selling Stealth 600 wireless gaming headset, dominates on PS5, PS4 & PS4 Pro, and features a new wireless mini-USB transmitter for a rock-solid connection. New for Stealth 600 Gen 2 is a larger flip-to-mute mic for refined chat clarity that integrates into the headset when muted, plus uniquely re-shaped, breathable ear cushions with ProSpecs technology provide glasses friendly comfort over long gaming sessions. Turtle Beach's data-proven Superhuman Hearing gives players the ultimate audio advantage, driven through precision-tuned 50mm speakers for a deeply immersive, undistorted audio experience. Plus, Stealth 600 Gen 2 is also Sony 3D Audio ready for PS5.
AGEECCQF4MDXAP7RJDDPHK4U54FQ.review_text: These work and look as good as new. Does not come with a USB dongle but it isn't needed. If for some reason you need it the replacement dongles are only 20$.
predicted_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity
AGEECCQF4MDXAP7RJDDPHK4U54FQ.user_history_review: Headset arrived looking as-new and powered on/paired fine. Just note it does NOT include the USB dongle/transmitter. You don't need it for normal use, but if you want a spare/replacement they're about $10-15 online. Setup was straightforward and it worked as expected.
user_history_themes: Hardware Build Quality Comfort and Connectivity
AGEECCQF4MDXAP7RJDDPHK4U54FQ.best_prediction_review: Headset arrived looking as-new and powered on/paired fine. Just note it does NOT include the USB dongle/transmitter. You don't need it for normal use, but if you want a spare/replacement they're about $10-15 online. Setup was straightforward and it worked as expected.
AGEECCQF4MDXAP7RJDDPHK4U54FQ.user_norm_context:
- Begin by noting the headset's as-new appearance and basic working status.
- State that a USB dongle is not included, but clarify it is unnecessary for normal operation.
- Add the approximate low cost of buying a replacement dongle for readers who still prefer to have one.
- Avoid commentary on audio quality, comfort, or long-term durability since these aspects were not personally evaluated.

leave_one_out_history_reviews.review_text: These work and look as good as new. Does not come with a USB dongle but it isn't needed. If for some reason you need it the replacement dongles are only 20$.
Works well. As described. For the original switch. Makes it easier to hold and more comfortable.

I have tried several headsets for the XBOX and always get complaints of reverb. I own both the 700 max gen 2 and the 600 max gen 2. No more complaints of reverb. Battery life is amazing. These sets are super comfortable. Both are easy to connect to the XBOX wirelessly. If I had to choose one over the other I would choose the 600 over the 700 I find it to have a better fit My husband would choose the 700 over the 600 so lucky for us we have both. Would recommend.

The match-3 is still fun and the decorating choices are cute, but progress slows to a crawl unless you play constantly. It kills quick pick-up-and-play.

I like the game, but...
1. Being part of a resort is a complete waste. You can help out with helicopter orders, but half the time you have to reboot the game, before you can visit anyone. 2. You can't sell anything to each other, exclusively, especially when someone on another continent needs something. Someone else always gets it. WHY is there not a resort exclusive sale slot?
3. Cruise events take all of one or two ingredients, so that you have none to do anything else. The quantity needed, for the minimal crafting points, is ridiculous.
4. Leader board events are a waste of time. Unless you plan on spending money, you never win. Quantity on these are to high here also.
5. You only ever have 6 permanent people to gather ingredients. If you request a guest, you rarely ever get one.
This game needs improved.
